L1.area: Input sanitization and request filtering logic

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled input submitted through a web form is insufficiently sanitized before being processed by the server. Backtick characters are not stripped from the input, allowing an attacker to inject and execute arbitrary operating system commands via shell metacharacter expansion.

L3.area: Core request security filtering and input sanitization module for user-submitted form data
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists where user-controlled input submitted through a video embedding form is insufficiently sanitized. The input sanitization routine strips single quotes but does not strip backtick characters, allowing an attacker to inject and execute arbitrary operating system commands via shell backtick expansion when the input is processed server-side.

ground_truth.reason: The input sanitization in the security filtering module strips single quotes but not backtick characters, allowing an attacker to embed shell commands via backtick expansion in the video link field, achieving remote code execution.
